title: Fix typing of dynamic `request` attribute on `Failure` with a cast subclass
body:
This PR addresses a longstanding `TODO` in the `call_spider_async` method regarding the typing of the dynamically added `request` attribute on `twisted.python.failure.Failure` objects.

Since `Failure` does not originally define the `request` attribute, adding it dynamically causes static type checkers (e.g., mypy) to raise errors.

To resolve this without changing runtime behavior or introducing new `Failure` instances, this PR introduces a lightweight subclass `FailureWithRequest` used solely for static typing purposes via `cast()`. This approach:

- Provides type safety and clarity for static analysis tools  
- Avoids creating new `Failure` instances, preserving error context and traceback  
- Keeps runtime behavior unchanged  
- Offers a balanced solution (middle ground) between ignoring type checks and a full refactor of `Failure` usage

step_plan.summary: Replace the `type: ignore` workaround for the dynamically added `.request` attribute on Twisted `Failure` objects by introducing a typing-only `FailureWithRequest` subclass and using `cast()` in the scraper’s failure path, preserving the original Failure instance and runtime behavior.

steps.operation: Introduce a typing-only Failure subclass and replace the dynamic `.request` assignment ignore with a `cast()` in the scraper failure path
steps.reason: `twisted.python.failure.Failure` does not define `.request`, so dynamically attaching it forces `# type: ignore[attr-defined]`. Declaring a lightweight `FailureWithRequest(Failure)` (typing-only) and using `typing.cast` before assignment/access makes mypy accept the code while preserving the exact same runtime object (no new Failure instance, traceback/context preserved).
steps.side_effects: No runtime behavior change intended (still mutates the same Failure instance by adding `.request`). Static typing improves; any downstream code relying on `# type: ignore` can be cleaned up later. Ensure imports (`typing.cast`, `TYPE_CHECKING`, and `Request`) don’t introduce runtime-only dependencies.

steps.operation: Validate and (if necessary) minimally adjust how Failures are created/forwarded into the scraper to remain compatible with the new cast-based typing approach
steps.reason: This is the contract point that can inject a plain `Failure()` into the scraper path (`scrape_func(Failure(), request)`); confirming that no code starts depending on `isinstance(..., FailureWithRequest)` and that the cast-based approach remains purely static ensures the typing fix in `call_spider_async` doesn’t conflict with how failures enter the pipeline.
steps.side_effects: Likely no functional changes required; at most, add/adjust local type hints/comments to document that the `.request` attribute is attached later in the scraper. Be careful not to change failure construction semantics or wrap/replace the Failure instance, which could affect traceback preservation.
